title: LatticeWave Support
preview: Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signups already created trial org (3 developers). Primary use: chat-based customer support + internal doc search and answer summarization. POC ask: 4-week trial, ~5k messages/month, expected concurrency ~15-25 QPS during peak. Latency target: 95th percentile < 300ms for short chat respon...
text: Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signups already created trial org (3 developers). Primary use: chat-based customer support + internal doc search and answer summarization. POC ask: 4-week trial, ~5k messages/month, expected concurrency ~15-25 QPS during peak. Latency target: 95th percentile < 300ms for short chat responses (prompt + 256 tokens). Cost sensitivity: high — finance asked for per-conversation pricing estimate and recommendations for batching/caching. Security: legal asked for SOC2 Type II, full sub-processors list, and a DPA template to review. Also asked about data residency (prefer US only), audit logs retention (90 days), and whether Redwood uses any third-party NLP enrichment vendors. Quote from Head of Eng: "We can move fast if SOC2 and DPA look clean — small ARR but rapid scale potential if cost/perf works." AE notes: prospect is open to hosted API only (no VPC/private) ...

text:
Discovery notes (concise):
- Inbound from content marketing campaign (signup form + demo request).
- Company: BryteBridge — SMB marketing platform that auto-generates campaign content (multi-tenant SaaS for small brands).
- Primary ask: high-volume, low-cost text generation for daily newsletter + social + landing page variants.
- Volume: estimated 8M tokens/month in steady state; initial POC target = 1.2M tokens/month.
- Latency/throughput: p50 target <250ms for single prompt flows used by editor UI; bulk batch jobs run asynchronously (throughput 200-400 reqs/sec during batch windows).
- Cost sensitivity: high. Current vendor spend ~ $2.5k/month; want better unit economics at scale.
- Technical preference: open-models/quantized variants acceptable; interested in Redwood's model catalog + quantization guidance.
- Critical features called out: prefix/KV caching for template-heavy conten...

Low-latency chat (target p95 < 600ms for short prompts) and cost predictability are must-haves.

Signed up via docs and quickstart; no AE outreach yet. Key points: prefers hosted API (fast time-to-market), will likely stick with public cloud. Quote from call: "We need something that 'just works' — latency under ~250ms p50 for short chats, and simple token billing we can pass to customers." Budget: very cost-sensitive; target < $1k/mo initially, with willingness to ramp if product-market fit. Wants to test smaller open models first (Llama-family, distilled) but may use Redwood-verified models if cost/perf is right. text:
Inbound from product-led signup. Sees immediate value in embeddings for semantic search and chat assistant for merchant support.

Primary contact: CTO - Maya Patel (also handles infra/security). Quote: 'We need SOC2 evidence before procurement but can trial hosted API for 30 days.'

Latency/throughput: target p95 < 300ms for short chat turn, expect ~40-60 concurrent agents during business hours.

Model preference: cost-conscious; prefer quantized open models or small LLMs for initial deployment. Will test 2-3 variants.

Routing/fallback: interested in automatic fallback to cheaper model when load spikes; want easy pinning per API key.

Data handling: transactional data may include last4 PAN and customer notes => ask for redaction guidance and retention controls.

Security asks: SSO (SAML) integration, SOC2 report (type II) timeline, audit logs, KMS integration for key control. Data re...

title: BerryTrail AI
preview:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-h...
text:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-hallucinating answers for SKU/spec info.
What we heard on 3/9 call:
- "Customer quote: 'We need reliable answers about SKUs and specs — hallucinations cost us returns.'"
- Current infra: frontend + search on AWS; wants hosted_api (public cloud) to prototype quickly, then evaluate Dedicated later if traffic grows.
- POC scope: embeddings for 1M product text tokens (catalog + specs + reviews), reranker for top-10, chat for post-search follow-up. Expected POC volume: ~50k queries/month.
- Latency targets: embeddings batch job OK to be minutes; chat interactions need p95 <= 250m...

Prospect inbound from self-serve trial (signed up via quickstart). Early discovery call 2026-03-02. Primary product interest: hosted API for call-summary + downstream lead scoring. Key ask: PII handling and retention. Wants option to redact/scrub PII at ingest and configurable retention windows (30/90/365 days).

Call snippets (rep paraphrase): "We need to be sure transcripts with customer emails/cc numbers do not leak into embeddings or long-term storage." Said they will test with 1000 weekly calls. Cost sensitivity moderate — SMB budget. Latency target: median generation <800ms for 1.2k token conversations; willing to accept up to 1.5s for batched summaries.

Security: requested SOC2 report, KMS support, audit logs; asks about retention controls exposed in Console (UI + API). Legal sent initial questionnaire (attached on Drive).

Notes on integration: prototyping with webhooks + Red...

Inbound SMB — discovered via blog post on cost-saving prompts.

Primary contact: Nora Patel (Head of Product) — small team, tight budget.

Use-case: chat-first customer support assistant, limited fallback to doc search and summarization.

Current infra: public cloud (AWS), no VPC/private deployment requested — wants self-serve hosted.

Key ask: how to reduce monthly token bill without hurting answer quality.

"Token audit" requested — wants breakdown by route, top 10 prompts, cost per intent.

Expressed interest in: batching, prefix caching, prompting compression, model/temperature tuning.

Quote from Nora on 2026-03-03 call: "We need predictable bills — last month spiked 3x on support hours."

SE notes (Marcus): initial logs show heavy context windows on 'order history' flow — possible to cache KV and compress prompts.

Immediate next: run usage export and token audit; prepare 2 opti...

text:
Lead source: inbound via blog post about low-latency chat widgets. Founder (Maya Rao) did initial sign-up and created API key but hasn't started high-volume calls. Small engineering team (2 engs) building an AI-first in-product assistant for their B2B dashboard.

Founder call summary (2026-03-05):
- Maya (founder/CTO) on call with AE + SE; very price sensitive — wants to understand real per-1M-token cost including caching/batching benefits.
- "We compare prices with OpenAI/Anthropic — but we also care about predictable latency and regional availability."
- Use-case: interactive chat + code snippet generation in app; average prompt length ~150 tokens, responses ~250 tokens. QPS expected low (0.5-2 rps) but spiky when customers onboard.
- Prefers hosted API for now (no infra ops), may consider Dedicated when ARR grows.
- Wants a simple calc: monthly dev/dev+prod token burn examples and ...

text: Summary: Enterprise private (VPC) deployment use-case: low-latency inference for capital-markets support platform; must satisfy strict EU data residency and auditability. Primary asks: 1) SSO/SAML integration with Okta, group-to-role RBAC mapping in Redwood Console/control plane; 2) SCIM provisioning so HR/Identity can auto-provision/deprovision users and groups; 3) KMS integration (customer-managed keys, key rotation, optional HSM) and ability to encrypt model cache and at-rest blobs; 4) Audit logging forwarded to their SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls; 5) Private control plane reachable only over peered VPC or VPN, no internet-exposed control endpoints.

title: Cobalt Nimbus LLC
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometim...
text:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometimes emits function_call events in multiple chunks. The Node SDK handler receives first chunk containing 'function_call' and partial 'arguments' string; later chunks complete it. Customer's parser tries to JSON.parse on first visible chunk and fails with 'Unexpected token' errors. Also observed cases where model returns single quotes instead of double quotes inside arguments ("TypeError: Unexpected token '\' in JSON at position 0"). They attempted a server-side workaround to buffer until end_of_stream but that increases latency and complexity.

Customer also reports that when...

title: LittleBay Helpers
preview:
SMB inbound. Small product team (Customer Success automation startup). Primary asks: low-latency chat for support agents, embeddings for knowledge base search, and cheap reranking for triage. Wants public cloud hosted API (self-serve) — will consider Dedicated later if volume grows.

Model selection questions are th...
text:
SMB inbound. Small product team (Customer Success automation startup). Primary asks: low-latency chat for support agents, embeddings for knowledge base search, and cheap reranking for triage. Wants public cloud hosted API (self-serve) — will consider Dedicated later if volume grows.

Model selection questions are the most active thread: they repeatedly asked for guidance on Llama vs Mistral vs Qwen and tradeoffs between 7B/13B/14B families. Key quotes: "Can we get chat-like quality at ~1/3 the cost?" and "We need p50 latency <300ms for short responses, p95 <800ms when longer."

Current thinking from customer: try Mistral-Instruct-7B for low cost, Llama-2-13B for highest helpfulness on instruction prompts, Qwen-14 if bilingual support needed (EN/CN). Concerned about token cost and tail latency on larger models. Asking about quantized variants and whether Redwood's hosted kernels give b...

text:
Inbound via doc-search blog post; signed up for free tier and created first embedding index. Quick notes from discovery call: 
- CTO (Maya Singh) is primary technical contact; wants hosted API to avoid ops overhead.
- Primary workload: legal document search + contract Q&A (RAG). Low QPS (10-30 reqs/min) but need fast reranking for top-k (p95 latency target 150-250ms).
- Currently evaluating vector DBs: Pinecone + Weaviate; asking about recommended approach for reranking pipeline (embeddings -> ANN -> rerank via Hosted API LM).
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, expects usage to start small; asked about token pricing + embedding unit definitions.
- Model preference: open models preferred (Llama2 style), but open to Redwood-curated models if latency/cost are better.
- Security: requires SSO for dashboard, audit logs for queries, and ability to set retention to 90 days for PII. Not on-prem;...

text: CRM notes (shorthand) - inbound SMB lead; primary contact: Maya Reed (Head of Product). Wants a fast POC: searchable knowledge base + conversational support widget embedded in Vercel site. Tech stack: Next.js on Vercel, backend serverless functions, some Python scripts for offline embedding jobs. Key asks: 1) quickstart showing LangChain + LlamaIndex connecting to Redwood hosted API (examples for both Node and Python), 2) Vercel AI SDK adapter snippet that uses streaming responses from Redwood, 3) guidance around batching/embedding cost vs latency, recommended model variants and quantization suggestions from Redwood Optimize, 4) note on rate limits and best practices for token caching. Maya quote from 3/7: 'Need something we can ship in two weeks — show me code, not slides.'

Quote from founder: "latency under 250ms for completions is a hard constraint for UX". Prefers public cloud — not ready for Dedicated or Private. Cost-sensitive; wants guidance on batching + token estimates. Hit default quota limit after ~300 reqs/day (trial). Saw occasional 429s and one 503 during spike; wants SLA info for production. Interested in embeddings later for repo search. No AE touch yet, just automated welcome flow. Wants simple example for streaming to UI and a snippet showing cost estimate per completion. Will consider upgrade to paid monthly once initial plugin has 100 DAU.

CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).

text:
CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).
- Primary pain: strict regulatory + residency requirements across US & EU, must be able to run inference in customer VPC.
- Key ask: Private VPC deployment with SSO (SAML/Okta), SCIM provisioning for automated user/groups, and fine-grained RBAC exposed in Redwood Console/control plane mapped to AD groups.
- Security stack: customer uses AWS (GovCloud for some workloads) + Splunk for SIEM. They require: customer-managed KMS keys (CMK), audit log streaming to Splunk/CloudWatch, 7-year retention for logs, and SOC2/ISO27001 evidence.
- Quote from CISO: "No console admin user that isn't bound to our AD groups — full RBAC and SCIM mapping is non-negotiable."
- Technical notes from SE call: need SAML metadata template, SCIM provisioning test plan, sample mapping for role -> AD group ...

title: BeaconFlow Labs
preview:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SL...
text:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SLOs: p50 target ~120-200ms, p95 target <500ms for interactive onboarding steps; streaming + function-calls must be consistent (users notice variance).
- Cost controls: token cost caps per onboarding session (target <= $0.15/session initially), need per-route token caps and throttles; interested in Optimize recommendations (batching/caching) to reduce unit cost.
- Tooling: must support function/tool calling to internal product metadata + CRM (tokenized access), streaming partial responses to UI while tool calls resolve.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 posture important, KMS for ke...

text:
Quick CRM notes, rough and chatty.
- Company: mid-market ecommerce platform focused on post-purchase experience (returns + shipping).
- Primary pain: high touch manual returns chats, CS costs spike during seasonal events (Nov-Dec). Want to automate ~60% of inbound returns flows w/ safe tool-calling to order service.
- P95 latency is a gating metric: they want reliable p95 for user-facing assistant. Target we discussed: first-token streaming <= 200ms, end-to-end p95 for short-turn responses (<=120 tokens) <= 600ms under normal load; tolerate higher during brief peaks if fallback works.
- Throughput: typical steady-state ~25-40 RPS, expected seasonal peaks up to 350-500 RPS per region (NA). Concurrency: ~120 concurrent live chats typical.
- Model preferences: prefer open/LLM variants they can run on Dedicated later (Llama-2-Chat style), but open to Redwood hosted for POC for speed. Want...

title: Emberfield KnowledgeFlux
preview:
Quick CRM notes / call snippets:
- Business: Emberfield builds a knowledge-layer SaaS used by ~300 mid-market customers for in-app help & support automation.
- Current stack: Elasticsearch + FAISS for embeddings, custom cross-encoder on CPU for rerank (high cost, unpredictable tail latency).

text:
Quick CRM notes / call snippets:
- Business: Emberfield builds a knowledge-layer SaaS used by ~300 mid-market customers for in-app help & support automation.
- Current stack: Elasticsearch + FAISS for embeddings, custom cross-encoder on CPU for rerank (high cost, unpredictable tail latency).
- Problem: want to reduce per-query cost and tighten p95 latency at scale (peak traffic: ~600 QPS, avg 200 QPS). Index size currently ~350k canonical docs (mixed product docs + transcripts) and growing ~30%/yr.
- Desired architecture: hosted vector search (ANN) + Redwood-hosted reranker (cross-encoder) with configurable fallback to lightweight scoring. Goal: p95 end-to-end rerank+retrieve <200ms, target median <120ms.
- Cost targets: business wants cost-per-query (embeddings + rerank) <= $0.005 on average; willing to trade small latency increase for 2x cost reduction vs current infra.
- Routing/fa...

title: Elm Harbor Assistants
preview:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal test...
text:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal testing):
- Users in US experiencing ~600-900ms extra RTT vs EU users; account originally created with default region eu-west-1.
- Frontend shows request timeouts at ~12s (client-side) when prompts include long context + history (~1.5-3k tokens).
- Team tried increasing client timeout but worried about cost and UX.

What they've asked/said: 
- "We signed up fast, but users are saying the bot feels slow — is this something we can fix without moving infrastructure?" 
- Very cost-sensitive; prefer hosted API but may upgrade if clear perf wins.

Redwood quick wins suggested on call...

title: Flashpoint LLM Economics
preview: Flashpoint runs per-impression rerank + personalization where latency is business-critical. They need a low-latency inference path with cost-aware controls: batching profiles for high throughput windows, KV/prefix caching to avoid repeated compute on session context, and quantization options that preserve ranking qu...
text: Flashpoint runs per-impression rerank + personalization where latency is business-critical. They need a low-latency inference path with cost-aware controls: batching profiles for high throughput windows, KV/prefix caching to avoid repeated compute on session context, and quantization options that preserve ranking quality. They want Redwood Optimize to propose batching/caching/quantization configs and a perf benchmark showing p50/p95 and cost deltas to validate a dedicated capacity commitment.

title: Emerald Bridge Integrations
preview: Summary: midsize SaaS platform that resells and integrates disparate helpdesk tools for retail clients. Primary ask: robust tool-calling to create/update tickets, trigger refunds via internal REST APIs, and escalate to L3 via Slack/Jira. POC scope narrow: Zendesk create/update + refund endpoint + automated escalatio...
text:
Summary: midsize SaaS platform that resells and integrates disparate helpdesk tools for retail clients. Primary ask: robust tool-calling to create/update tickets, trigger refunds via internal REST APIs, and escalate to L3 via Slack/Jira. POC scope narrow: Zendesk create/update + refund endpoint + automated escalation flow.

Requirements & constraints:
- Tool-calls must be idempotent and show audit trail (who/what/when).
- Refund flow goes through internal refunds API that touches PCI-adjacent services — legal demands scoping and tokenization; cannot store raw PAN.
- Must provide per-request trace IDs and tie AI-decisions to logs for 7+ months (audit_logging).
- EU customers require residency; prefer control plane in public_cloud but data plane in VPC/EU region.
- Burst reliability: peak 5x normal traffic for 20-40 minutes (holiday sale spikes). Need graceful degradation (fallback to q...

text: Workloads: chat suggestions in merchant dashboard (low latency), embeddings for fast product search, reranking for fraud signals.,Latency target: p95 <= 250ms for text-gen endpoints at expected load (burst to 60 req/s).,Throughput: steady-state 15-25 req/s, burst capacity up to ~60 req/s for peak events.,Model prefs: open-weight models acceptable; want guidance on 7B vs 13B tradeoffs and quantization recommendations.,Routing/fallback: automatic fallback to smaller/cheaper model for non-critical paths; need canary for model rollouts.,Security: Okta SSO/SAML mandatory, SOC2 evidence to satisfy procure/legal, audit logs retained 90 days minimum.,Deployment plan: start hosted API for 6-8 week POC, evaluate Dedicated/VPC if data residency or performance demands rise.

- Mid-market expansion: onboarding 30 new customers in last 12 months.
POC summary (Hosted API trial):

text:
Account background:
- Founded 2018, product analytics SaaS for B2B marketplaces. Heavy emphasis on conversational product support and embedded search in product UI.
- Mid-market expansion: onboarding 30 new customers in last 12 months.
POC summary (Hosted API trial):
- Trial window: 2026-01-12 -> 2026-02-28. Ran support-bot + search rerank workloads against 200k session dataset.
- Observed traffic: avg QPS (user-facing inference): 3.2 QPS; typical daytime baseline 5–7 QPS; peak bursts up to 28 QPS sustained for ~90s during product launch tests.
- Concurrency: observed 18 concurrent streams during peaks; 24 concurrent warm KV-cached sessions in internal stress tests.
- Token mix: median prompt tokens 380, median completion tokens 120 -> average total ~500 tokens per call. Token distribution: ~76% prompt-side tokens (document context + metadata), 24% generation tokens.
- Workload split ...
